channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Nothing off table in picking Clarke successor - SFA
item.description: The Scottish FA's chief executive says "nothing's off the table" in replacing Steve Clarke as national head coach, a process that "starts now".

item.title: Who do Scotland fans want as the next head coach?
item.description: The speculation has started, but who do Scotland fans want to replace Steve Clarke?

item.title: How Bellingham became Tuchel's most important player
item.description: His starting place was the subject of much debate in Thomas Tuchel's England team, but Jude Bellingham has become integral to the manager's style of play.

item.title: Ugarte has 'most serious injury footballer can face'
item.description: Manchester United have confirmed that midfielder Manuel Ugarte suffered knee ligament damage during Uruguay's World Cup defeat by Spain.

item.title: Are Iran the unluckiest side in World Cup history?
item.description: Iran had a place in the last 32 of the World Cup snatched away from them at the last minute not once but twice.

item.title: England labour past Panama to qualify for last 32 as group winners
item.description: Goals from Jude Bellingham and Harry Kane help England secure a 2-0 win over Panama, as Thomas Tuchel's side qualify for the World Cup last 32 as winners of Group L.

item.title: Knockouts are here - what is England's path to the final?
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at what England will need to do to reach the final after qualifying for the knockout stages.

item.title: France the team to beat? Chris Sutton predicts World Cup's last-32 matches
item.description: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the last-32 ties at the 2026 World Cup, including England against DR Congo.

item.title: Rice set for England start against DR Congo
item.description: Declan Rice is expected to be available to start England's World Cup last-32 tie with DR Congo on Wednesday.

item.title: 27 flights, 24 matches: The carbon cost of Fifa president's World Cup tour
item.description: Gianni Infantino has clocked up thousands of air miles across North America so far.

item.title: Clarke leaves strong Scotland legacy but exit brings sense of relief
item.description: Steve Clarke took Scotland out of the wilderness - but not past the group stage of a tournament. Tom English's must-read analysis of his reign.

item.title: Maestro Messi scores again and breaks another record in the process
item.description: Lionel Messi comes off the bench to score his sixth goal of the 2026 World Cup as Argentina beat Jordan 3-1 in Dallas and becomes the first player to score in seven World Cup games in a row.

item.title: Austria score dramatic late equaliser against Algeria as both progress
item.description: Algeria and Austria produce an incredible finish to their Group J meeting, with both teams scoring in injury time to secure the point they need to reach the World Cup knockout stages - and send home Iran.

item.title: Algeria and Austria both qualify for last 32 after dramatic injury-time goals
item.description: Algeria and Austria each score injury-time goals in a 3-3 draw, a result which sees both sides qualify from Group J and into the last 32 of the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Messi comes off the bench to score as Argentina beat Jordan
item.description: Lionel Messi comes off the bench to become the first player in FIFA World Cup history to score in seven consecutive matches, as Argentina defeat Jordan 3-1 in their final game in Group J.

item.title: 'Huge relief' - Bellingham puts England ahead against Panama
item.description: Jude Bellingham does enough to get just ahead of his defender and poke the ball home from Bukayo's Saka corner and put England ahead against Panama.

item.title: Derided by Leeds, snubbed by USA - but Marsch becoming Canada hero
item.description: Derided by Leeds fans and snubbed by the USA, Jesse Marsch has faced difficult moments in his career - but appears to have found his home with Canada as they look to reach the World Cup last 16.

item.title: What you need to know about England's opponents DR Congo
item.description: England will face DR Congo in the round of 32. Here is what you need to know about them.

item.title: England last-32 tie on BBC - see full schedule
item.description: England's World Cup last-32 game against DR Congo will be shown live on BBC One and BBC iPlayer, with all 16 first knockout round ties covered across BBC Sport.

item.title: Big names, dark horses and subplots - reasons to watch each last 32 tie
item.description: BBC Sport shows why you should be interested in all of the World Cup's last-32 ties.

item.title: World Cup goals chart led by PSG and Real - plus Palace and Sunderland
item.description: Crystal Palace and Sunderland lead the Premier League’s World Cup scoring charts after the 2026 group stage, ahead of Arsenal, Liverpool and Manchester City.

item.title: How to win a World Cup penalty shootout
item.description: We have a look at what can be learned from every World Cup penalty taken in a shootout ever.

item.title: Where to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
item.description: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup knockout games.

item.title: Catch up on the World Cup without any spoilers
item.description: It is a dilemma plenty of fans in the UK will have - and we are giving you the chance to follow the World Cup without spoilers.

item.title: Will Clarke successor be big-name overseas coach or Scotland icon?
item.description: The key questions around the appointment of Steve Clarke's successor - and some of the potential candidates.

item.title: Job done - but England will not win World Cup unless they improve
item.description: England did their job topping the group - but they can forget winning the World Cup if they do not improve, writes Phil McNulty.

item.title: New deal, new beginnings & a sudden exit - Clarke's Scotland timeline
item.description: BBC Scotland charts the trajectory of Steve Clarke's last month as Scotland head coach.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 21
item.description: Will you get today's World Cup player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: Best team, moments, matches and players of World Cup group stage
item.description: As the World Cup group stages come to an end, BBC Sport reporters pick their highlights so far.

item.title: Kane becomes England's top World Cup scorer - and now chases other greats
item.description: Harry Kane became England's all-time leading World Cup scorer with his 12th goal in the win against Panama on Saturday, but how does he compare to other country's greats?

item.title: Tuchel worried about right-back situation
item.description: Thomas Tuchel says he is "worried" about England's right-back crisis after Jarell Quansah became the latest in the position to suffer injury.

item.title: Colombia denied late winner in enthralling draw with Portugal
item.description: Colombia and Portugal play out one of the games of the tournament, as both sides qualify for the World Cup last 32 following a gripping 0-0 draw.

item.title: DR Congo come from behind to book last-32 tie with England
item.description: DR Congo set up a World Cup last-32 meeting with England by reaching the knockouts for the first time as they come from behind to beat Uzbekistan in their final group-stage game.

item.title: 'Get rid of VAR!' Rooney unhappy Sanchez goal ruled out
item.description: BBC Sport World Cup pundit Wayne Rooney cannot believe Davinson Sanchez's goal for Colombia against Portugal was ruled out because the defender was offside by a toe.

item.title: Lo Celso marks his World Cup debut with a fantastic free-kick
item.description: Giovani Lo Celso scores a fantastic free-kick on his World Cup debut to give Argentina the lead against Jordan.

item.title: 'He's that guy!' Pundits praise 'brilliant' Bellingham
item.description: BBC Sport World Cup pundits Micah Richards and Wayne Rooney praise Jude Bellingham's "desire and hunger" to perform on the biggest stage, after the England midfielder produces a goal and an assist in England's 2-0 win over Panama.

item.title: Kane and Bellingham proved again why they are key players - Tuchel
item.description: Thomas Tuchel says Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham proved once again why they are key players for England, after both men scored in a 2-0 win over Panama which sees Tuchel's team win Group L at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Croatia beat Ghana to reach knockout stages
item.description: Nikola Vlasic scores a late header as Croatia booked their place in the World Cup last 32 with victory over already-qualified Ghana in Philadelphia.

item.title: Como bid for Chelsea's Chalobah - Sunday's gossip
item.description: Chelsea are handed a bid from Como for defender Trevoh Chalobah, Everton are frontrunners to sign Blues striker Liam Delap, while Inter Milan may miss out on Liverpool midfielder Curtis Jones over his valuation, plus more.

item.title: Salah an injury doubt for Egypt's last-32 tie
item.description: Egypt captain Mohamed Salah is a doubt for Friday's World Cup last-32 tie against Australia after suffering a hamstring injury.

item.title: 'Bye-bye, Scotland' - Clarke's leaving letter to Tartan Army
item.description: Steve Clarke's letter to the Scotland fans in full following his departure as head coach.

item.title: Spain's Williams criticises tackle that injured him
item.description: Nico Williams criticises Uruguay's Nicolas de la Cruz for a "completely unnecessary" tackle that injured the Spain winger.

item.title: One night in Miami: The Scotsmen of the apocalypse
item.description: BBC Scotland's Scott Mullen paints the scene from Miami as Scotland's World Cup hopes fade.

item.title: Who had the X-factor and who had a tough game? England player ratings
item.description: BBC Sport's England reporter Alex Howell rates the players after the Three Lions' win over Panama in the World Cup.

item.title: Man Utd waiting on Ugarte injury after he leaves pitch on stretcher
item.description: Manchester United are waiting to discover the extent of Manuel Ugarte's injury after the midfielder was stretchered off during Uruguay's World Cup defeat by Spain.

item.title: Sunderland reject £8m Chelsea bid for Xhaka
item.description: Sunderland say experienced midfielder Granit Xhaka is not for sale after rejecting an £8m bid for the player from Chelsea.

item.title: Gakpo asks for privacy after loss of unborn son
item.description: Noa van der Bij, the partner of Liverpool and Netherlands forward Cody Gakpo, announces the pair have lost their unborn son.

item.title: Spain's Pino may miss rest of World Cup
item.description: Spain winger Yeremy Pino may miss the rest of the World Cup with a suspected broken collarbone.

item.title: Fuchs leaves Newport County after just seven months
item.description: Newport County are hunting a fourth manager in little more than two years ahead of the 2026-27 season.

item.title: Why are World Cup underdogs doing so well?
item.description: Are the surprise results achieved by lower-ranked teams in the World Cup a matter of luck - or clever planning and execution?

item.title: Why it's time for Saka and Rashford - Shearer
item.description: Former England captain Alan Shearer explains why Thomas Tuchel needs to change some personnel as well as his team's mindset when they face Panama.

item.title: Tuchel's England are opposite to Southgate's - and built to beat top teams
item.description: BBC Sport's football tactics writer Umir Irfan explains why Thomas Tuchel's England system is built to prosper against the world's top nations.

item.title: World Cup's best matches, moments, goals & players so far - ranked
item.description: After two rounds of fixtures, BBC Sport ranks the best matches, moments, players and goals of the World Cup so far.

item.title: Quiz: How well do you know birthday boy Messi at World Cups?
item.description: Argentina great Lionel Messi turns 39 today and has been lighting up the World Cup this summer, but how well do you know his journey so far?

item.title: The beauty of sharing your child's first World Cup
item.description: There is nothing like the first World Cup you can enjoy with your kid - and the wide-eyed wonder through which they see it.

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What is BBC's 3D Live Match Experience and how does it work?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the unique way of following matches during the 2026 World Cup via the BBC

item.title: Why Saudi Arabia and Iraq flags are not laid down at World Cup
item.description: The flags of Saudi Arabia and Iraq are not laid on the ground during World Cup pre-match ceremonies because they include religious wording.

item.title: Will three points guarantee a spot in World Cup knockouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at whether earning three points in the World Cup group stage will be enough to advance to the knockout rounds.

item.title: What are World Cup's red card suspension rules?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains Fifa's suspension rules on red and yellow cards during the World Cup.

item.title: How to watch highlights of World Cup matches
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how you can watch match highlights and full match replays during the 2026 Fifa World Cup

item.title: Why are footballers wearing pink boots at the World Cup?
item.description: The opening match of the 2026 World Cup was dominated by players wearing pink boots - but why is the colour so prevalent in football at the moment?

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: Rangers close in on defender Godfrey - gossip
item.description: Serie A defender set for loan to Rangers as MLS clubs step up chase for out of contract Hearts midfielder...

item.title: Are Celtic reacting quickly enough to 'wake-up call'?
item.description: Have Celtic learned lessons from last season as Martin O'Neill sounds "wake-up call" warning?

item.title: Why Vrancken marks fresh start at data-driven Hearts
item.description: As Belgian head coach Wouter Vranckin takes charge at Hearts, it marks a strengthening of the club's data-driven vision, writes Thomas Duncan.

item.title: Pattinson signs new deal with London City Lionesses
item.description: London City Lionesses has announced England defender Poppy Pattinson has extended her contract until summer 2029.

item.title: Double Ballon d'Or winner Putellas to join London City Lionesses
item.description: Two-time Ballon d'Or winner Alexia Putellas decides to move to London City Lionesses after leaving Barcelona.

item.title: Graham agrees permanent deal with Ipswich Women
item.description: Ipswich Town Women sign forward Kit Graham on a two-year contract following a successful loan from Tottenham last season.

item.title: Tottenham sign former Arsenal midfielder Pelova
item.description: Tottenham confirm the signing of Netherlands midfielder Victoria Pelova following her exit from Arsenal.

item.title: Godfrey signs new three-year deal with London City
item.description: England international Freya Godfrey has signed a new three-year contract with London City Lionesses.

item.title: Lazio to pay compensation in pregnancy dispute case
item.description: Lazio Women have been ordered to pay compensation to former midfielder Maja Gothberg because they unlawfully ended her employment due to pregnancy.

item.title: Brighton in advanced talks to sign Walti
item.description: Brighton are in advanced talks to sign Juventus and Switzerland midfielder Lia Walti.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.
